methods.description: Construction of a data matrix for 78 species-level taxa, measured for 16 quantitative and semi-quantitative characters of the dental apparatus, to analyze functional richness and evenness.

steps.description: A data matrix including these 78 species, measured for 16 quantitative and semi-quantitative characters of the dental apparatus, was constructed.
steps.searchable_summary: construct data matrix for 78 species with 16 dental characters

steps.description: Reconstruct the full data set for the taxon from several incomplete specimens when possible.
steps.searchable_summary: reconstruct data set from incomplete specimens

steps.description: Use data from Recent specimens for conspecific taxa with living species.
steps.searchable_summary: use recent specimen data for conspecific taxa

steps.description: Reconstruct data from congeners for extinct species.
steps.searchable_summary: reconstruct data from congeners for extinct species

methods.description: Analysis of functional richness and evenness using correspondence analysis and various statistical methods to understand niche space occupation and taxon distribution.

steps.description: Measure functional richness by taking the area of the convex hull enclosing all species in the space of the first two axes of a correspondence analysis.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate convex hull area for functional richness

steps.description: Calculate functional evenness as the evenness of taxon distribution in filled niche space.
Present the distribution of taxa in the space of the first two correspondence axes with kernel plots.
steps.searchable_summary: create kernel plots for taxon distribution

steps.description: Calculate functional evenness using mean and variance in distance between points, nearest neighbor analysis, and Ripley's K.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate functional evenness with multiple methods

methods.description: Perform statistical calculations to analyze the data using PAST software and visualize results with Aabel software.

steps.description: Perform statistical calculations using PAST ver. 2.10.
Create all graphs using Aabel ver. 3.0.5.
